Geopolitics.summary: Millions gathered in the holy city of Karbala, Iraq to pay their respects at the funeral of Iran’s late supreme leader.

Geopolitics.title: Nato allies announce £37bn for new missile project
Geopolitics.summary: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer will convene around a dozen leaders to discuss the programme in Ankara.

Geopolitics.title: Indonesia’s free meals programme plagued by alleged corruption and waste
Geopolitics.summary: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto's initiative has been criticised for misdirecting resources to affluent regions.

Geopolitics.title: Pet prescriptions could be capped at £21 under proposed vet sector reforms
Geopolitics.summary: Ministers also considering licence requirement and regulator to try to cut bills and increase choice UK vets may have to have a licence and cap prescriptions for pet medicine at £21 under plans being considered by the government. Ministers are also considering establishing a regulator for the veterinary sector, including inspections, a mandatory licensing system and published compliance reports to improve accountability and choice. Every vet practice could need an official operating licence – similar to GP surgeries and care homes – under proposals in a white paper. Continue reading...

Geopolitics.title: 'The water just came so fast': Typhoon triggers floods and rare tornadoes in China
Geopolitics.summary: Villagers whose families have been stranded told the BBC they are struggling to get food and help.

Geopolitics.title: Family demands investigation after US man killed by ICE agent in Texas
Geopolitics.summary: Calls for answers after Lorenzo Salgado Araujo fatally shot during traffic stop in Houston in latest ICE killing.

Geopolitics.title: US moves to delist Syria as a ‘state sponsor of terrorism’
Geopolitics.summary: President Trump announces a move to delist Syria, citing efforts by Ahmed al-Sharaa to unify the country.

Geopolitics.title: Deadly strikes in Gaza leave 5 dead, children wounded
Geopolitics.summary: Israeli strikes in Khan Yunis and Gaza City killed five people and wounded others, including children.
